description_long: This is a mid-rise office building with a symmetrical facade clad in red brick and featuring a regular grid of white-trimmed windows. The front elevation displays five vertical rows of windows, each with a consistent rectangular shape and white framing, and a recessed central section at the roofline. The ground floor includes a prominent double-door entrance flanked by large windows, suggesting a lobby or reception area. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with evenly spaced windows and uninterrupted brickwork, lacking the decorative detailing of the front. The roof is flat, with a small penthouse or mechanical room set back from the main facade. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on function and repetitive architectural elements typical of late 20th-century urban office buildings.
description: A red-brick office building with a grid of white-trimmed windows, a flat roof, and a prominent double-door entrance on the front facade. The design is symmetrical and utilitarian, with repetitive window patterns and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with five vertical rows of white-trimmed windows, a recessed central roof section, and a double-door entrance at the ground level.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain brick wall with a single vertical row of evenly spaced windows and a small penthouse structure at the roof.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a uniform brick surface with a grid of windows and a small central penthouse, lacking any entrance or decorative features.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with a single vertical row of windows and the penthouse structure visible at the roofline.
attribute_window_pattern: Regularly spaced, vertically aligned rectangular windows in a grid pattern with white trim on all facades.
